slides.title: How setState Works 🔄
slides.content:
# setState

`setState` is a method in `State` class that notifies Flutter that the internal state has changed, triggering a rebuild of the widget.

* Call `setState(() { ... })` with the code that modifies state.
* Only the widget and its descendants rebuild.
* Keep the callback synchronous and quick.

quizData.question: What happens when you call setState?

options.text: The widget's build method is scheduled to run
The widget is removed from the tree

quizData.explanation: setState marks the widget as dirty and requests a rebuild.

slides.title: Using setState Correctly
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Don't call setState after disposal

Always check `mounted` before calling setState in asynchronous callbacks to avoid errors.
